question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter(e -> e.getType() == EntityType.TOPIC) check in topicExists() by supplying a topicId that resolves to an entity whose type field is null, causing a NullPointerException that bypasses the entity-type validation and allows streaming of fee messages from non-topic entities?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gapFilter construction (topicContext.getFilter().toBuilder().startTime(startTime).build()) in the safety-check path to set startTime to last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1, which could be a timestamp that precedes a fee-schedule message, causing the gap-recovery query to retrieve and re-deliver that fee message?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.merge(safetyCheck, live) non-deterministic ordering to receive a live fee-schedule message before the safetyCheck gap-recovery completes, causing the onNext() sequence-number check to throw IllegalStateException and abort the subscription mid-fee-update?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the startTime default value (DomainUtils.now() at filter construction time) by constructing a TopicMessageFilter with a startTime that is slightly in the past relative to a fee-schedule message's consensusTimestamp, causing the historical retriever to include that fee message in the stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, filter.getEndTime()) conversion in isComplete() by supplying an endTime value that causes an ArithmeticException (overflow) in the nanosecond conversion, causing isComplete() to throw and the subscription to terminate before delivering a fee-schedule message?
